aesthetic_dna: American luxury with romantic optimism — Tiffany Blue as emotional shorthand for love and celebration in China. Democratic luxury bridging accessible silver entry and investment-grade high jewelry, repositioning under LVMH since 2021 from traditional bridal to aspirational contemporary luxury for Chinese millennials and Gen Z.
current_direction: HardWear as edge and modernity for self-purchase; Lock as the new iconic statement piece; T collection as everyday accessible luxury. Repositioning from bridal-only to a full-life luxury brand across gifting, self-reward, and investment categories for younger Chinese consumers.
brand_voice: Optimistic, romantic, celebratory, warm — American warmth not European aloofness. Never intimidating, always inviting. Celebrates love in all its forms. Emotionally generous rather than exclusive. The Blue Box is the message.

client_archetypes.name: 初入奢品 Chū Rù Shēpǐn
client_archetypes.lifestyle: University graduate or early-career professional in a Tier 1 Chinese city. First major luxury purchase is a milestone of self-achievement.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Owning something real and recognizable — the Blue Box is proof of arrival. Wants a piece she can wear every day that signals taste without over-signaling wealth.
client_archetypes.occupation: Junior professional, graduate student, entry-level finance or tech
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 入门奢品 / 人生第一件 / 每天都能戴 / 显白 / 少女感 / 送自己
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Anything ostentatious or that tries too hard — gaudy stones, heavy gold, anything that screams wealth rather than taste
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The Blue Box moment — the unboxing is the experience. Internationally recognized, a brand her peers will know.

client_archetypes.name: 都市精英 Dūshì Jīngyīng
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Senior professional or entrepreneur with established career. Upgrades from silver entry to gold and diamond. Jewelry marks professional and personal milestones.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Jewelry that grows with her career — from the T wire she bought at 24 to the T1 gold ring she wears to board meetings. Tasteful, international, investment-quality.
client_archetypes.occupation: Senior manager, lawyer, financial analyst, startup founder, consultant
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 低调奢华 / 投资价值 / 百搭 / 职场精英 / 精致生活
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Trendy fast-fashion jewelry, overly bridal styling outside a wedding context
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The brand's global recognition and the clean American aesthetic — understated power. The T collection reads as both modern and timeless.

client_archetypes.name: 爱的见证 Ài De Jiànzhèng
client_archetypes.lifestyle: In a relationship milestone — engagement, wedding anniversary, or celebrating love. Views Tiffany as the definitive love jewelry brand.
client_archetypes.aspiration: The Tiffany Setting solitaire is the dream. Wants a ring that every Chinese woman will recognize as a serious commitment.
client_archetypes.occupation: Various — the occasion defines this persona, not the career
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 求婚必备 / 爱的承诺 / 一生一次 / 永恒 / 蒂芙尼蓝
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Lab-grown diamonds, generic diamond rings without brand heritage, bridal jewelry from non-luxury brands
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The Tiffany Setting is the most famous engagement ring in the world. The Blue Box proposal scene is embedded in Chinese romantic culture via film and social media.

client_archetypes.name: 礼物策划 Lǐwù Cèhuà
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Corporate gifter, relationship manager, or senior male buyer choosing jewelry as a relationship investment. Budget is secondary to the impression made.
client_archetypes.aspiration: The gift that cannot be misinterpreted — Tiffany communicates seriousness, taste, and care.
client_archetypes.occupation: Business owner, senior executive, sales director, relationship manager
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 体面礼物 / 送得出手 / 有面子 / 不踩雷 / 七夕礼物
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Jewelry with no brand recognition, anything that requires explanation, fashion jewelry
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The Blue Box removes all ambiguity. The recipient will know what this means before opening it.

client_archetypes.name: 自我犒赏 Zìwǒ Kǎoshǎng
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Self-purchase driven by personal achievement, emotional milestone, or simply because she wants it. Rejects the idea that jewelry must be given.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Jewelry on her own terms, on her own timeline. HardWear — edgy, modern, no-romance-required — is her natural home.
client_archetypes.occupation: Creative industry, media, marketing, tech, design
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 送自己 / 凭什么等别人送 / 独立女性 / 自我奖励 / 戴上就是态度
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Anything that reads as 'waiting to be gifted' — overly bridal, overly delicate, or passive in aesthetic
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The brand's self-purchase narrative is increasingly explicit. HardWear speaks directly to her — confident, modern, intentional.

client_archetypes.name: Z世代潮流收藏 Z Shìdài Cháoliú Shōucáng
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Gen Z collector who treats luxury accessories as cultural artifacts and identity signals. Deeply XHS-native, trend-aware, and brand-literate beyond her price range.
client_archetypes.aspiration: The Return to Tiffany heart tag is her cultural reference, not just a necklace. Wants the piece she's seen on the celebrities she follows.
client_archetypes.occupation: Student, part-time creator, junior creative, supported by parents
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 经典复刻 / 小众but有范 / 博主同款 / 蒂芙尼蓝 / 显白神器
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Anything corporate, adult, or 'mom-coded'; obvious fakes or dupes in social settings
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The Return to Tiffany moment — the heart tag is a cultural icon she grew up seeing. The Blue Box photo is made for XHS.

client_archetypes.name: 男性购买者 Nánxìng Gòumǎizhě
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Male buyer purchasing for a female partner, fiancée, wife, or mother. Jewelry is unfamiliar territory — he needs guidance and confidence.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Get the gift right the first time. The Blue Box is proof of effort and taste even when he has no jewelry vocabulary.
client_archetypes.occupation: Finance, tech, business owner, corporate manager
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 送女友必看 / 七夕礼物推荐 / 求婚选什么 / 她一定喜欢
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Anything that requires him to defend the choice — must be universally recognized as quality
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: The Blue Box eliminates decision anxiety. 'I bought her Tiffany' requires no further explanation in any Chinese social context.

client_archetypes.name: 跨境收藏 Kuàjìng Shōucáng
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Frequent international traveler or overseas Chinese who compares prices across markets. Price-conscious but quality-committed.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Get the authentic piece at the best global price while maintaining the full Tiffany experience.
client_archetypes.occupation: Business traveler, overseas student returnee, finance/consulting
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 海外价格 / 免税购买 / 价格对比 / 正品保证
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Daigou with uncertain authenticity, any piece without full brand documentation
preferred_collections: High-end pieces with significant price difference
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: Heritage since 1837, global price consistency relative to other LVMH brands, strong resale recognition.

client_archetypes.name: 投资意识 Tóuzī Yìshí
client_archetypes.lifestyle: Investment-aware luxury consumer who tracks resale values, collectibility, and brand trajectory. Treats jewelry as wearable asset allocation.
client_archetypes.aspiration: Pieces that hold value or appreciate — especially Blue Book and limited pieces. The Tiffany Setting is the most liquid engagement ring on the secondary market.
client_archetypes.occupation: Finance, investment, wealth management, business owner
client_archetypes.trend_language_that_resonates: 保值 / 升值潜力 / 拍卖行 / 蓝皮书 / 限量款
client_archetypes.what_they_would_never_buy: Fashion jewelry, silver (insufficient investment grade), anything without strong secondary market
client_archetypes.what_makes_them_choose_tiffany: Brand liquidity on the secondary market — Tiffany Setting is the most recognized engagement ring globally and trades accordingly.

entry_silver: Return to Tiffany heart tag necklace (¥3,800)
investment_diamond: Tiffany True ring in platinum with diamonds (¥55,000)

entry.ca_angle: The Blue Box experience at an accessible entry. 'This is her first Tiffany — let's make it memorable.'

core.ca_angle: Gold-tier commitment. 'She's ready to move from silver to gold — this piece she'll wear to every important meeting.'

aspirational.ca_angle: Diamond category — occasion-defining. 'This piece marks a moment she'll remember for life.'

investment.ca_angle: Blue Book and bespoke — for clients who want the exceptional. By appointment, with full gemological certification.

aesthetic_pillars.name: Blue Box Romance
aesthetic_pillars.description: The Tiffany Blue box is the world's most recognized luxury packaging — in China it has become emotional shorthand for love, celebration, and arrival.

aesthetic_pillars.name: American Optimism
aesthetic_pillars.description: Warm, democratic luxury that invites rather than excludes — accessible at entry yet aspirational at high jewelry, without European aloofness or intimidation.

aesthetic_pillars.name: Iconic Forever
aesthetic_pillars.description: Return to Tiffany, T, HardWear, and Lock are modern icons designed to outlast trends — pieces worn across decades and occasions, not just for one moment.

aesthetic_pillars.name: Love Language
aesthetic_pillars.description: Tiffany speaks the universal language of connection — from first love to lifelong commitment to self-love — with HardWear as the statement for those who write their own story.

competitive_differentiation.Cartier: Tiffany leads on emotional resonance with milestone moments and Chinese romantic culture; Cartier signals European status while Tiffany signals American warmth. Tiffany wins on gifting occasions; Cartier wins on status display.
competitive_differentiation.Van Cleef & Arpels: Tiffany offers more accessible entry without sacrificing aspiration; VCA is perceived as more exclusive and associated with older established wealth. Tiffany wins with younger buyers.
competitive_differentiation.Bvlgari: Tiffany is more romantic and feminine versus Bvlgari's bold Italian statement aesthetic. Both appeal to self-purchase but Tiffany wins decisively on engagement and gifting occasions.

clienteling_tone: warm, optimistic, celebratory, personal — speak to the occasion and the emotion, not just the product

category_cadence.rings: evergreen — engagement driven by relationship milestones, fashion rings by self-purchase
category_cadence.bracelets: seasonal peaks at Valentine's, Qixi, CNY gifting windows
category_cadence.necklaces: entry-level purchase driven by daily wear trend, XHS everyday luxury content
category_cadence.earrings: underrepresented in current XHS discourse — opportunity to develop
category_cadence.engagement_rings: spike at marriage proposal season (spring/fall) and Valentine's periods
